channel.title: My AI Filtered News
channel.description: Cleaned news feed via Gemini AI

item.title: Vance heads to Pakistan for talks to end the war between Iran, the US and Israel
item.description: Vice President JD Vance enters his highest-profile moment Friday as lead U.S. negotiator in face-to-face talks with Iran.

item.title: Inflation surges to highest level in nearly 2 years as energy costs spike
item.description: Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years. Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.

item.title: Five things to know about Hungary's election
item.description: Hungary votes Sunday in a pivotal test of Viktor Orbán's "illiberal democracy," as challenger Péter Magyar taps voter frustration, with stakes for Europe, NATO and the U.S.

item.title: Israel threatens healthcare as it continues to attack Lebanon
item.description: Israel threatens healthcare as it continues to attack Lebanon

item.title: Vance expects ‘positive’ Iran talks as he heads to Islamabad
item.description: US VP JD Vance says Trump gave “clear guidelines” for Iran talks in Pakistan and expects “positive” negotiations.

item.title: Artemis II splashdown: When it lands, risks and how to watch live
item.description: The Orion spacecraft will make its return to Earth at 00:07 GMT, splashing down off the San Diego coast.

item.title: Pakistan sets modest goal for US-Iran summit: A deal to keep talks going
item.description: US-Iran talks open in Islamabad with mediators focused on keeping dialogue alive amid deep divisions.

item.title: Current price of oil as of April 9, 2026
item.description: When oil prices change, it affects your energy costs—and even the price of everyday items. Here’s why.

item.title: ‘Babies become sitting ducks’: Babies too young for vaccines remain vulnerable in measles ‘hotbed’ communities
item.description: Trump administration officials have been pushing anti-vaccine experts in statehouses.

item.title: CoreWeave’s New Junk Bond Jumps Amid Meta, Anthropic Deals
item.description: CoreWeave Inc.’s $1.75 billion junk bond has rallied amid increased optimism about the cloud-infrastructure firm thanks to deals with some major technology companies.

item.title: Here Are the Key Takeaways From the US CPI Report for March
item.description: Here are the key takeaways from the US consumer price index report for March, released Friday:

item.title: As De Beers Bid Deadline Looms, Botswana Pushes for Control
item.description: Botswana is holding to its ambition of buying a controlling stake in De Beers from Anglo American Plc, potentially dampening interest from some private investors looking to acquire the diamond giant.

item.title: Ares Plans a Smaller Private Credit Fund With Less Leverage
item.description: Ares Management Corp. is planning a significantly smaller flagship US direct lending fund than its previous record-breaking vehicle of $33.6 billion to speed up the deployment of capital amid broader dislocations in private markets.

item.title: Trapped French Property Investors Find an Exit, at a Price
item.description: As a growing number of French property funds freeze withdrawals in the face of a client exodus that’s overwhelmed their cash buffers, the true cost of liquidity is becoming clearer.

item.title: Bank Stocks Have Worst Start to Year Since 2023 as Results Loom
item.description: With war raging in the Middle East and private credit fears shaking markets, shares of Wall Street’s biggest banks are off to their worst start to a year since the regional banking crisis. But with the stocks now trading at relatively cheap valuations, strong earnings next week could spark a rally.

item.title: Switzerland Aims to Finalize US Tariff Accord by the End of July
item.description: Switzerland aims to finalize a US trade deal by the end of July, with the European nation pushing for assurances it won’t face higher duties than rival countries.

item.title: Surveillance Firm Hawkeye 360 Files for IPO in Defense Tech Boom
item.description: Hawkeye 360 Inc. filed for an initial public offering, joining a string of defense technology firms seeking to go public this year amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Stocks Extend Rally for Eighth Day on CPI Data, US-Iran Talks
item.description: US equities extended gains as investors cheered inflation data that came roughly in line with expectations and weighed whether a fragile truce between Washington and Tehran can hold.

item.title: Goldman’s ‘Unwavering’ Support Shows Gulf’s Lure for Wall Street
item.description: Late last month, Blackstone Inc. announced the first inbound Gulf private equity deal since Iran started attacking Middle Eastern hubs, while Citigroup Inc.’s top boss fired off a 600-word memo underlining the bank’s enthusiasm for its business in the region.

item.title: Stocks Eye Best Week Since May on US-Iran Hopes: Markets Wrap
item.description: The first inflation snapshot of the war-fueled spike in energy costs matched expectations, driving stocks higher ahead of this weekend’s planned peace talks between the US and Iran.

item.title: US Consumer Sentiment Drops to Record Low on Price Concerns
item.description: US consumer sentiment fell in recent weeks to a record low, indicating Americans’ increasing worries about mounting inflation due to the Iran war.

item.title: Consumer Sentiment Falls to a Record Low on Inflation Concerns
item.description: The preliminary April consumer sentiment index slumped to 47.6 from 53.3 in March, according to University of Michigan data. The survey period includes responses from March 24 to April 7. That was below all but one estimate in a Bloomberg survey of economists. Mike McKee reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Vista Credit Sets Sights on Hard-Hit Software Loans Via New Fund
item.description: Vista Equity Partners’ credit-investing arm is raising $250 million for a new fund that will buy the beaten-down debt of software companies, seeking to capitalize on recent selloffs tied to concerns over artificial intelligence.

item.title: 追踪高市早苗首相（10日）
item.description: <上午>07:53 抵达官邸。08:00 出席中东局势相关阁僚会议。08:17 会见财务相片山皋月。08:21 出席内阁会议。08:29 会见外相茂木敏充。09:00 会见国家安全保障局长市川惠一、内阁情报官原和也、外务省综合外交政策局长有马裕、防

item.title: Texas court overturns sentence for man on death row for nearly 50 years
item.description:
Clarence Curtis Jordan was convicted in 1978 but hadn’t had a lawyer for over 30 years

The Texas court of criminal appeals has overturned the death sentence of Clarence Curtis Jordan, a 70-year-old man with intellectual disabilities, who spent nearly 50 years on death row – much of that time without a lawyer.

Jordan was convicted in 1978 for the murder of Joe L Williams, a 40-year-old grocer in Houston, and was sentenced to death. In the years that followed, courts determined that Jordan, who has intellectual disabilities, was “incompetent”, making him ineligible for execution under constitutional standards.

Continue reading...

item.title: US should judge UK on its actions in Gulf, not on Trump’s social posts, says defence secretary
item.description:
UK military bases have been invaluable to US, says John Healey, and Britain’s efforts ‘speak for themselves’

  • Middle East crisis live – latest updates

The UK’s actions in the Gulf should be the basis for any US judgment of the country’s value rather than Donald Trump’s social media posts, Britain’s defence secretary has said.

Speaking at a conference in London, John Healey said the UK’s recent efforts “spoke for themselves”, as Keir Starmer flew home after a trip that included discussing how to keep the strait of Hormuz open with Donald Trump, as well as meeting leaders in Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain.

Continue reading...

item.title: ’He’s mentally unstable’: Iranian American in Congress condemns Trump’s war and pushes for his removal
item.description:
Democratic representative Yassamin Ansari says the war has only more deeply entrenched the Iranian regime

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Donald Trump is an “evil human being” who “wants to be an emperor” and should be removed from office over the war in Iran, Yassamin Ansari, an Iranian American member of the US Congress, has told the Guardian.

Ansari, the daughter of Iranian immigrants who decades ago fled the regime, spoke out after the president threatened to wipe out Iran’s civilisation before backing down and announcing an uncertain two-week ceasefire.

Continue reading...

item.title: European airports could face jet fuel shortages within three weeks
item.description:
Summer flights and holidays may also be at risk, says the Airports Council International

European airports have said jet fuel shortages could hit the summer holiday season, if oil supplies do not start to flow through the strait of Hormuz within the next three weeks.

Airports Council International (ACI) Europe wrote to Apostolos Tzitzikostas, the EU transport commissioner, saying the bloc is three weeks away from shortages.

Continue reading...

item.title: Human rights groups decry US plan for Guantánamo camp for Cuban migrants
item.description:
Exclusive: Dozens of organizations write to Congress after general announced plan to ‘deal with’ those fleeing any humanitarian crisis on the island

Dozens of US and international human rights organizations are decrying the Trump administration’s plans to establish a migrant “camp” for fleeing Cubans at the Guantánamo Bay military base if the island nation’s crisis worsens under pressure from the US, according to a letter to members of Congress on Friday.

The 85 groups plan to submit the joint letter, exclusively shared with the Guardian, to US senators and House representatives, expressing their “profound concern” with comments made last month by a top Department of Defense commander, and describing any prospect of further migrant detention at the base as “deeply troubling and unacceptable”.

Continue reading...
